title: Single-pax single-segment domestic AC400 YYZ→YUL — INT, with CM-feed CUSTOMER_PROFILE attach AND missing FDM flight_leg parent
captured_from: trip-tracer Aurora PG (INT) materialised end-state via SSM bastion ac-cct-kafka-client-int-cac1

classification.description: Single-pax single-segment same-day-departure PNR_CREATION at AC Montreal desk; CM-feed publishes a SEGMENT/INITIAL with regulatory-checks payload referencing the PNR segment; passenger gets a CUSTOMER_PROFILE id from Entity Resolution within 35s of creation; EDS emits a single bound APPR for YYZ→YUL with 4 trailing null-bound re-evaluations. NB the upstream FDM feed has NOT created a flight_leg row for the referenced flight (AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25) — INT trip-tracer flight_leg only contains March-2026 AC400 dates. Any FDM event for this flight that arrives later will land on the cct-ingestion-fdm-fk-queue-int (per LEARNINGS §10 trap #16/17 and §12.6), waiting for the parent flight_leg to materialize.

event_fingerprint: fdm:NO flight_leg row exists for AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25

fdm_observations.fdm_topology_note: FDM has only TWO environments: TEST and PROD. Both CRT and INT trip-tracer DBs subscribe to the SAME FDM TEST stream — validated by near-identical received_at timestamps (CRT 03:29:14.096, INT 03:29:14.159 on 2026-04-25, 63 ms apart) and identical per-day flight_leg counts.

fdm_test_coverage.note_on_INT_extra_AC400: INT shows 17 March-2026 AC400 rows that CRT does not. Either INT received an additional historical replay/seed for those dates, or there is a small drift between the two consumer subscriptions of the TEST stream. The AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25 leg is absent from BOTH envs.

fdm_observations.implication: Any FDM event for AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25 that arrives later will fail the fk_flight_updates_flight constraint and route to cct-ingestion-fdm-fk-queue-{env} per LEARNINGS §10 traps #16-17. But the parent will likely never arrive in TEST — AC400 is not in the curated TEST flight set. To exercise FDM for AMQ9SH-related testing, either (a) seed AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25 manually into the TEST flight_leg upstream, or (b) use one of the flights that IS in the TEST set (AC2447, AC2353, etc.) when constructing test PNRs.
fdm_observations.recommended_followup: Confirm with the FDM owners which flights are intentionally in TEST. If AMQ9SH-style test PNRs need full FDM coverage, change PNR scenarios to book one of the carrier+flight numbers known to be in TEST.

timing.budget_notes: 44.4s trip→EDS is somewhat above the ~14s p99 transformer hop in CRT, but consistent for INT (lower throughput, smaller worker pool). Apr-2026 INT FDM gap means the regime APPR was emitted without a parent flight_leg ever existing in DB.

flight_leg.note: FDM upstream gap — no parent for the referenced flight

should_not_trigger: disruption detection workflow (no irregular operation; APPR regime is normal pre-departure)
should_trigger_if_fdm_arrives_later: ingestion-service-fdm: 23503 FK violation on insert into flight_leg_updates → routed to cct-ingestion-fdm-fk-queue-int
Eventually flight_leg_updates rows for the leg AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25 once a flight_leg parent is created (e.g. by OAG glue or backfill)

operational_findings.finding: FDM has only TEST and PROD environments — no per-env (INT/CRT/BAT) FDM streams
operational_findings.evidence: CRT and INT trip-tracer DBs receive flight_leg writes within 63 ms of each other (CRT 2026-04-25 03:29:14.096, INT 03:29:14.159). Per-day counts virtually identical. CRT total flight_legs spans 2024-01-10 to 2026-05-12 (1,138 rows total — clearly a curated TEST sample, not a production firehose).
operational_findings.impact: Any team treating the per-env FDM feed as isolated from CRT is mistaken — they share the upstream. A bad TEST flight_leg reaches both. A missing one is missing in both.
operational_findings.stated_by: Suresh (user) — '"FDM only has 2 environments, TEST and PROD"'

operational_findings.finding: AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25 is absent from FDM TEST entirely (not just INT)
operational_findings.evidence: CRT flight_leg has zero AC400 rows for any date. INT has 17 AC400 rows but only 2026-03-12..2026-03-31. Neither env has 2026-04-25.
operational_findings.impact: Any PNR in either INT or CRT that books AC400 for April 2026 will have FDM updates fail FK and accumulate in the fk-queue indefinitely.
operational_findings.likely_cause: AC400 is simply not part of the curated TEST flight set. Active TEST flights last 24h: AC2447, AC2353, AC2334, AC7181, AC1922, AC132, AC7171, AC2359, AC2074, AC2358 — AC400 not among them.
operational_findings.recommended_action: Either seed AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25 into TEST upstream, or have AMQ9SH-style synthetic PNRs book a flight that IS in TEST.

operational_findings.finding: INT has 17 AC400 March-2026 flight_legs that CRT lacks
operational_findings.evidence: Same FDM stream feeds both, yet INT shows 17 March-AC400 rows, CRT shows 0
operational_findings.impact: Either INT received an additional historical replay/seed not applied to CRT, or there is small drift between consumer subscriptions of the same TEST stream.
operational_findings.recommended_action: Worth a short investigation by the FDM team — confirm whether replay/seed jobs run against INT-only paths.

operational_findings.finding: EDS emitted bound APPR regime even with no parent flight_leg
operational_findings.evidence: eds_pnr_output rows show regime APPR with promisedSegments referencing a segment whose flight_id has no flight_leg row
operational_findings.impact: Regime classifier appears to operate on PNR-side data (flight_segment) without joining on flight_leg, which is correct for the imminent-departure rule but means it can't catch FDM-feed gaps as part of its own validation.
operational_findings.recommended_action: Treat as informational. If FDM-presence is required for downstream actions (e.g. disruption detection), add a separate consistency check.

provenance.method: psql via SSM bastion ac-cct-kafka-client-int-cac1 (i-0ebd041df525a972e) → ac-cct-trip-tracer-rds-proxy-int-cac1.proxy-czy2ye8u22qy.ca-central-1.rds.amazonaws.com:5432, user dbdevuser, db trip-tracer, table-by-table for pnr_id='AMQ9SH-2026-04-24'
kafka_topics_inferred_from_db: DERIVED-CM-EVENTS-INT (CM SEGMENT/INITIAL + LAST_MODIFICATION + REGULATORY_CHECKS — published by 1A internal trigger from LON1A0955)
EVENT-DETECTION-PNR-INT → trip + trip_details + passenger + flight_segment + passenger_updates rows 2-3
RESULT-EVENT-DETECTION-INT (EDS rule-engine output) → eds_pnr_output (5 rows, 1 with bounds + 4 trailing null-bound re-evals)
provenance.raw_kafka_payloads: not captured — only DB end-state. To reconstruct: pull DERIVED-PNR-EVENTS-INT filtered by event_log_id 43753b13bee1796ec8e0c06d8777d476-bd3dc6f18a5d3221, and DERIVED-CM-EVENTS-INT filtered by event_log_id e327e278cb84d0f2f5ee5ee4bc9631da-05d62be2bf625f97, both within ±2 min of 2026-04-24T22:00:00Z.

notes: Test-data signature: passenger name 'ROSS GELLER' is the Friends sitcom character. Likely a QA seed.

CM (Case Management / Customer Management) feed published a SEGMENT/INITIAL with full DCS passenger linkage (dcsPassengerId 610F762C00015FE1) and regulatory-checks payload covering 5 regulatory programs (ADC, AQQ-USA, iAPP-KOR, CANADIAN_PPP, PRIVATE_LIST). This is richer than the typical PNR-only flow seen in CRT scenarios — INT exercises the CM-driven enrichment path.

The 1A INTERNAL trigger from LON1A0955 (Amadeus office) suggests the test was kicked off via Amadeus tooling, not the AC native desk — even though the PNR was created at YMQAC010C. This is consistent with the CM feed being driven by 1A as the upstream controller.

passenger_updates row 1 (CUSTOMER_PROFILE attach) arrived ~35s after trip creation, faster than the EDS first-bound-emit (44s). Confirms the Customer-Profile flow in LEARNINGS §6.7 (Service Proxy customer-profile/search) is reachable for AMQ9SH.
The flight_segment.flight_id = 'AC#400#YYZ#2026-04-25' uses the standard FDM key pattern, so the join wired correctly — only the parent row is missing.

0 ticket_updates: this PNR appears to be an unticketed hold or the TKT feed in INT is empty (LEARNINGS §12.1 flagged ALTEA-TKT-UAT as upstream-empty). Mirrors the AMQ9SH state observed in the previous AMQ9SH lookup.
